headline: I build web software, fullstack systems, and practical IT support.
subhead: I am a developer and IT support person working across websites, dashboards, backend systems, and technical troubleshooting for projects that need to work in real situations, not only in a clean demo.
intro: I enjoy finding the line between a simple interface, a reliable backend, and the technical issues people run into every day. I care not only about whether something looks finished, but also whether it is useful, understandable, and easy to keep running.
philosophy: Good development is not just a clean codebase. To me, it is a way to understand a problem, organize the moving parts, and create something people can use without having to fight the system.

A lot of my work sits between software and support — dashboards, backend flows, maintenance bugs, personal projects, and the small technical issues that can slow people down. In these situations, calm problem-solving matters more than adding more features.

Development does not happen in perfect conditions. There may be unclear requirements, broken setups, limited time, or tools that need to be connected first. I see those constraints as part of the work. My goal is to move things forward without making the next step harder.

experiences.title: Developer and IT Support
experiences.organization: College, internship, client and personal projects

beyond: Technology does not end with code for me. I am also interested in games, music, hardware, PCs, and AI — areas where I can learn how people use systems, tools, and interfaces in their own way.

A lot of that curiosity feeds back into how I work. Building a PC, fixing a setup, testing an AI workflow, or learning from games all sharpen the same habits: patience, detail, and practical troubleshooting.
Projects like this portfolio remind me why I enjoy development in the first place: the mix of learning, technical problem-solving, and the satisfaction of turning a rough idea into something real.
